label: PI-010 satisfier — entity v0.5+ schema growth end-to-end (migration + access + vocab + REST + UI + tests + spec amendment). NOT a new entity type — grows the existing `entity` methodology entity additively per `methodology-schema-specs/entity.md` v1.1. Migration 0019 adds one new TEXT NULL column to the `entities` table (`entity_kind` with CHECK admitting NULL or one of person/organization/event/transaction/other) and extends `refs.relationship_kind` CHECK to admit one new outbound vocabulary kind (`entity_variant_of_entity` — first entity-to-entity edge kind in v2's vocabulary). vocab.py registers the new kind and adds an `_kinds_for_pair` clause for `(entity, entity)`. Access layer adds `_coerce_kind` validator + `kind` kwarg throughout the entity repository functions. References repository adds variant-specific guards: cardinality, self-reference rejection, one-step cycle rejection — mirroring the `field_belongs_to_entity` precedent from PI-004. API schemas grow by `entity_kind` field on POST/PUT/PATCH bodies. UI dialog schema adds Kind combo (blank sentinel maps to NULL); detail pane renders Kind as a read-only label. 17 new tests cover the five-value round-trip, default-NULL, invalid-value rejection, empty-string coercion, PATCH-null clear, PUT-clear semantics, variant edge round-trip, cardinality guard, self-reference and cycle rejection, and vocab registration. RESOLVES PI-010 via slice A's atomic edge+flip behavior. Test baseline 1876 → 1893 passed (+17), 0 regressions. Identifier re-keying per the parallel-sandbox contingency in CLAUDE.md: originally planned as SES-089 / CONV-059 / DEC-291-293; re-keyed to SES-090 / CONV-060 / DEC-301-303 because a parallel-sandbox architecture-review session has staked an unapplied claim on the SES-089 identifier block.

session.title: PI-010 satisfier — entity v0.5+ schema growth end-to-end; RESOLVES PI-010
session.conversation_reference: Claude Code orchestrator session at Doug's terminal, 2026-05-26. Doug invoked the orchestrator with two prompts: (1) review PI-010 and prepend an executive summary + execution plan to the description; (2) execute the plan, complete the requirements, write a proper close-out, update documentation. The orchestrator dispatched an Explore agent for Phase A signal gathering, made the three Phase B design decisions directly from the signal, implemented Phases C–F inline (the work was tightly interconnected and benefited from a single context), declared Phase G a no-op from Phase A signal, and authored Phase H close-out artifacts. Implementation landed as commit 3b326ce. Three decisions DEC-301/302/303 cover the Phase B trio. Identifier collision discovered at close-out authoring time: a parallel-sandbox architecture-review session had drafted but not yet applied ses_089.json claiming SES-089 / CONV-059 / DEC-291-300 / PI-061-073. Re-keyed this session's identifiers to SES-090 / CONV-060 / DEC-301-303 per CLAUDE.md's identifier-collision contingency for parallel-sandbox work (precedent: SES-077). No work_ticket authored — this session was driven by direct user prompts rather than a kickoff-md-file.
session.topics_covered: Execution of the 8-phase PI-010 plan landed on the description in this session's first prompt. (Phase A — Signal gathering) Explore agent inventoried entity records in the live engagement DB (only ENT-001 / ENT-002 smoke-test records, no production variant-suffix names), located entity.md v1.0 at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/, surveyed vocab.py (37 kinds, no existing entity-to-entity edge kinds), identified migration 0018 as the head and the complete-CHECK-rewrite pattern for adding new refs.relationship_kind values, validated the five-value `person | organization | event | transaction | other` enum against 15 sample entity names from the methodology corpus (no records required `other`; Campaign / Engagement showed dual event/transaction readings), and confirmed PI-060 is conditionally non-blocking because variants carry their own field roster under PI-004's shipped pattern. (Phase B — Design conversations) Three decisions: DEC-301 — variant mechanism is references-entity edge with new kind `entity_variant_of_entity`, many-to-one at source via access-layer cardinality guard, mirrors DEC-053's default posture, first entity-to-entity edge kind in v2's vocabulary; DEC-302 — `entity_kind` is TEXT NULL column with CHECK admitting NULL or one of the five enum values, no default, operator-deferred classification is the legitimate starter state per DEC-051's posture; DEC-303 — migration story is additive-only no-op, no rewrite of any v0.4 records (none had variant suffixes), CBM-redo goes directly to the v1.1 mechanism. (Phase C — Schema + access layer) Migration 0019_v0_5_entity_kind_and_variants.py adds entity_kind TEXT NULL column with CHECK constraint via batch_alter_table, extends refs.relationship_kind CHECK to admit entity_variant_of_entity. vocab.py adds ENTITY_KINDS frozenset, adds the new kind to REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS, adds the `_kinds_for_pair` clause for `(entity, entity)`. Entity model gains Mapped[str | None] entity_kind column with CHECK constraint via __table_args__. Repository adds _coerce_kind validator + kind kwarg on _new_entity_row / _insert_with_autoassign / create_entity / update_entity / patch_entity. References repository adds variant-specific guards: cardinality, self-reference rejection, one-step cycle rejection. (Phase D — API + MCP) EntityCreateIn / EntityReplaceIn / EntityPatchIn each gain entity_kind field; /entities router passes entity_kind through on POST and PUT. MCP tool surface unchanged — methodology entity CRUD goes through generic REST proxy methods which pass-through the new field automatically. (Phase E — UI) `_entity_schema.py` adds entity_kind FieldSchema as a combo with blank sentinel + five enum values; `entities.py` panel detail pane renders entity_kind as a read-only QLabel with `(unclassified)` fallback. Master-pane Kind column deferred. (Phase F — Methodology integration) entity.md amended v1.0 → v1.1 with full Revision Control entry and Change Log entry, sections 3.2.3 / 3.3.1 / 3.3.4 / 3.8.3 amended, new section 4 (Migration and Reversibility v1.1+) added. No interview-guide or conduct-framework amendments. (Phase G — Backfill) No-op confirmed. (Phase H — Close-out) ses_090.json + apply prompt + spec amendment + commit 3b326ce all land. Test suite: baseline 1876 → 1893 passed / +17 new (0 regressions). Migration 0019 applied to live CRMBUILDER engagement DB.
session.summary: PI-010 satisfier (entity v0.5+ schema growth) shipped end-to-end. RESOLVES PI-010 via slice A's atomic edge+flip behavior. NOT a new entity type — additive growth of the existing entity methodology entity per entity.md v1.1. One new TEXT NULL column (entity_kind, CHECK-constrained to NULL or one of person/organization/event/transaction/other) — DEC-302. One new outbound vocabulary kind (entity_variant_of_entity, entity → entity, many-to-one at source via access-layer cardinality guard; first entity-to-entity edge kind in v2) — DEC-301. Additive-only migration with no data backfill — no v0.4 production variant data existed — DEC-303. Existing v0.4 entity records survive intact with entity_kind NULL and zero variant edges. entity_status three-value lifecycle preserved verbatim. Master pane unchanged per the same posture as DEC-054's Domains-column deferral. Detail pane gains a Kind read-only label; Create/Edit dialogs gain a Kind combo. References-create dialog surfaces the new kind automatically for (entity, entity) via the cascading filter from RELATIONSHIP_RULES. Migration 0019 fully reversible per entity.md v1.1 §4.3. Test suite: 1876 baseline → 1893 (+17 new, 0 regressions). Identifier re-keying per CLAUDE.md's parallel-sandbox contingency: SES-089/CONV-059/DEC-291-293 → SES-090/CONV-060/DEC-301-303.
session.artifacts_produced: Single build commit 3b326ce0c306453190db8ab292205f329e33ac43. New file `crmbuilder-v2/migrations/versions/0019_v0_5_entity_kind_and_variants.py`. Modified files: `crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/access/models.py`, `crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/access/vocab.py`, `crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/access/repositories/entity.py`, `crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/access/repositories/references.py`, `crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/api/schemas.py`, `crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/api/routers/entities.py`, `crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/ui/dialogs/_entity_schema.py`, `crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/ui/panels/entities.py`, `tests/crmbuilder_v2/access/test_entity.py` (+13 new tests + column-shape expectation grown to nine), `tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_entities_panel.py` (detail-pane widget-ordering expectation extended), `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/entity.md` (v1.0 → v1.1 amendment). This close-out payload `ses_090.json` and its apply prompt `CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-090.md`. Migration 0019 applied to the live CRMBUILDER engagement DB; deposit-event log `dep_NNN.log` lands at apply time.
session.in_flight_at_end: PI-010 resolves on this apply. After this apply, entity.md sits at v1.1 with both variants and kind classification live. Follow-on v0.6+ candidates surfaced during signal gathering but not authored as PIs in this session: kind-inheritance from a parent variant (if a variant's kind is unset, should it default-render the parent's kind?); kind-informed Phase 3 field-shape defaults; kind-informed Phase 5 CRM-engine scoring; master-pane Kind column. The PI-060 interaction remains Open but is no longer blocking variant work. The API server is still running on pre-migration code (separate restart needed before live entity_kind / variant edges can be authored via REST); the close-out apply flow doesn't depend on the API knowing about entity_kind.

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-010 — entity v0.5+ schema growth (entity_kind + entity_variant_of_entity)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-010 — entity v0.5+ schema growth (entity_kind + entity_variant_of_entity)

Grows the existing entity methodology entity per
methodology-schema-specs/entity.md v1.1. Additive; preserves every
v0.4 field, the three-status propose-verify lifecycle, the eight
endpoints, and the master pane verbatim.

Migration 0019:
- Adds one TEXT NULL column to entities: entity_kind. CHECK admits
  NULL or one of five enum values (person, organization, event,
  transaction, other).
- Extends refs.relationship_kind CHECK to admit
  entity_variant_of_entity — first entity-to-entity edge kind in v2's
  vocabulary.
- Reversible.

vocab.py:
- Adds ENTITY_KINDS frozenset (five values).
- Adds entity_variant_of_entity to REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS.
- Adds _kinds_for_pair clause for (entity, entity).

Model, repo, schemas, router:
- Entity model gains Mapped[str | None] entity_kind column + CHECK.
- _coerce_kind validator normalises None/empty-string to None.
- create_entity / update_entity / patch_entity accept the new
  optional kind kwarg.
- EntityCreateIn / ReplaceIn / PatchIn schemas accept entity_kind.
- /entities router passes entity_kind through on POST and PUT.

References repo:
- POST /references with relationship=entity_variant_of_entity
  rejects self-references, second outbound edges from the same
  source (cardinality_violation), and one-step cycles. Deeper cycles
  remain operator-responsibility.

UI:
- Create + Edit dialogs gain a Kind combo via the new entity_kind
  FieldSchema.
- Detail pane gains a Kind read-only label.

Tests (+17 new, 0 regressions): five-value round-trip, default NULL,
invalid-value rejection, empty-string coercion, PATCH-null clears,
PATCH change, PUT-clear semantics, variant edge round-trip, variant
cardinality guard, variant self-reference rejection, variant
one-step cycle rejection, vocab registration sanity, ENTITY_KINDS
shape check, detail-pane ordering. Baseline 1876 → 1893 passed.

PI-010 resolves on close-out apply.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

decisions.title: Entity variant mechanism — references-entity edge with `entity_variant_of_entity` kind; many-to-one at source via access-layer cardinality guard
decisions.context: PI-010 / Phase B1 of the orchestrated execution plan. The Mentor Contact / Client Contact pattern from CBM history needs first-class representation in v0.5+ — variants share a base type but carry different fields and role semantics. PI-010 named three candidate mechanisms: (A) references-entity edge with new vocab kind, (B) self-referential FK column on the entity table, (C) separate variant entity type. DEC-053 establishes references-edge as the default for methodology-entity many-to-many; the question for PI-010 is whether the variant relationship has a substantive reason to deviate. Phase A signal: no production variant data existed at decision time (live engagement DB had only smoke-test entities); PI-004's shipped field schema attaches fields per-entity via `field_belongs_to_entity`, so variants are field-roster-independent.
decisions.decision: Adopt option A: references-entity edge with new vocabulary kind `entity_variant_of_entity` (source `entity`, target `entity`). First entity-to-entity edge kind in v2's vocabulary. Mechanical additions per CLAUDE.md line 48: (1) `entity_variant_of_entity` added to `REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS` in `vocab.py`; (2) `_kinds_for_pair` extended with a clause for `(entity, entity)`; (3) Alembic migration 0019 extending the `refs.relationship_kind` CHECK. Cardinality many-to-one at source: an entity may have AT MOST ONE outbound `entity_variant_of_entity` edge. Enforced at the access layer via `references.py` `create()` guard, mirroring PI-004's `field_belongs_to_entity` precedent. Self-references rejected with `self_reference` code; one-step cycles (B→A when A→B exists) rejected with `cycle_violation`. Deeper-cycle prevention is operator-responsibility for v1.1.
decisions.rationale: Option B (self-referential FK) rejected: adds schema-table surface and prevents the inverse query ('what entities are variants of this entity?') from sharing the unified references-query path. Option C (separate variant type) rejected as overkill — the references table already does this with one new vocab kind. Option A wins on three grounds: (i) DEC-053 establishes references-edge as the default for methodology-entity many-to-many and there is no substantive reason to deviate (the variant relationship is structurally many-to-one but references handles that with an access-layer cardinality guard); (ii) option A reuses the existing `ReferencesSection` widget with zero new widget code; (iii) option A exercises the cascading-filter infrastructure with the first entity-to-entity kind cleanly.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Option B — self-referential FK column `entity_parent_identifier` (rejected; schema-table surface; inverse query becomes a table-scan). Option C — separate `entity_variant` entity type (rejected; the references table is the established v2 mechanism per DEC-006). DB-layer cardinality via partial UNIQUE on `(source_type, source_id, relationship_kind) WHERE relationship_kind='entity_variant_of_entity'` (rejected for v1.1; access-layer enforcement matches the PI-004 precedent). Deeper-cycle prevention at edge-create time (rejected for v1.1; graph walks add complexity; one-step guard catches the most common operator-error).
decisions.consequences: An entity has zero or one outbound `entity_variant_of_entity` edges; a base has zero or many inbound such edges. The `ReferencesSection` widget renders both directions. The cascading dialog surfaces `entity_variant_of_entity` and `supersedes` for `(entity, entity)` (plus generic `is_about` / `references`). Soft-deleting either endpoint does not cascade-delete the variant edge. Variants are field-roster-independent — PI-060 becomes informational rather than blocking. CBM-redo Phase 1 will exercise the mechanism with real variant cases; any friction surfaces as v0.6+ candidates.

decisions.title: Entity kind classification — `entity_kind` TEXT NULL column with five-value CHECK-constrained enum + operator-deferred NULL
decisions.context: PI-010 / Phase B2 of the orchestrated execution plan. DEC-051 deferred `entity_kind` to v0.5+ because base-type classification is Phase 3 territory and v0.4's thin shape stayed neutral about CRM-engine specifics. PI-010 named the candidate v0.5+ shape: an enum field on the entity table with values `person | organization | event | transaction | other`, informing Phase 3 field-shape defaults and Phase 5 CRM-engine evaluation scoring. The remaining shape questions for this build: column type, nullability, default value, validation enforcement layer, render strategy.
decisions.decision: Add `entity_kind` to the `entities` table as a TEXT column, NULL-able, no default. CHECK constraint admits NULL or one of the five enum values. Validation enforced both at the database layer (CHECK constraint) and at the access layer (`_coerce_kind` validator normalises None/empty-string to None; non-empty values must be members of `ENTITY_KINDS`). PATCH-null and PATCH-empty-string both clear the field; PATCH-omit leaves it unchanged. PUT replaces wholesale (omitted-from-body deserialises to None and clears the field). UI dialog presents a combo with a blank sentinel as the first option followed by the five enum values; the blank maps to NULL via `_coerce_kind`. Detail pane renders the value as a read-only label with `(unclassified)` fallback.
decisions.rationale: Operator-deferred classification (NULL) is the legitimate default because Phase 1 sometimes surfaces an entity before its kind is settled — same DEC-051 posture of 'don't force methodology concepts Phase 1 doesn't always produce'. Forcing a non-NULL value at create time would push operators toward `other` or guess-classifications that decay the signal value. The five-value enum is exhaustive for the CBM-redo-era methodology; Phase A's clustering check placed every of 15 sample entities confidently into one of the four substantive values with `other` as the safety sentinel. CHECK constraint at the DB layer plus `_coerce_kind` at the access layer is belt-and-braces per the DEC-006 vocabulary discipline. Empty-string-coerces-to-NULL handles the UI's blank-sentinel without requiring the combo to track a distinct 'no value' state.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Non-NULL required at create time with `other` as the default (rejected; pushes operators toward early-binding classifications). Two-axis classification (`entity_kind` + `entity_subkind`) (rejected for v1.1; subkind taxonomy undocumented). Free-text `entity_kind` with optional vocab guidance (rejected; loses CHECK-constraint validation). Default value `person` (rejected; silently biases the population). Length cap on the column (rejected; enum is closed so length is bounded). Kind-inheritance for variants (rejected for v1.1; deferred to v0.6+ as a CBM-redo signal candidate).
decisions.consequences: The entities table grows by one TEXT NULL column. v0.4 records survive intact with `entity_kind` NULL. Phase 3 work that depends on kind-classification reads the column and gates on NULL. Master pane does not gain a Kind column in v1.1 — operator-density tradeoff matches DEC-054's Domains-column deferral. Detail pane renders Kind as a read-only label; Create/Edit dialog presents it as a combo. Downstream Phase 5 CRM-engine evaluation will weight scores by kind once that automation lands (currently kinds are stored and rendered but not yet score-weighted — v0.6+ candidate). `other` is a safety sentinel; if CBM-redo surfaces a sixth natural cluster, a v0.6+ migration adds it.

decisions.title: Entity variant migration story — additive no-op for v0.4 records; no rewrite of `entity_name` suffix-named legacy variants
decisions.context: PI-010 / Phase B3 of the orchestrated execution plan. The v0.4 workaround for entity variants was to suffix `entity_name` (e.g. 'Contact — Mentor', 'Contact — Client') per entity.md v1.0 §3.3.4. With the v1.1 variant mechanism (DEC-301) landing, the question is whether the migration should rewrite legacy suffix-named records to attach explicit `entity_variant_of_entity` edges, or leave them untouched and let the new mechanism be additive-only going forward.
decisions.decision: Migration 0019 is additive only — no rewrite of any v0.4 records. Operator-visible upgrade ergonomics: legacy suffix-named records remain operator-editable, and operators may either (a) attach explicit `entity_variant_of_entity` edges between the records leaving the names unchanged, or (b) rename the variant records and attach edges. Either pattern is forward-compatible. No automation rewrites names or attaches edges at upgrade time.
decisions.rationale: Phase A signal confirmed no production variant data existed at decision time — the live CRMBUILDER engagement DB held only two smoke-test entities (ENT-001 / ENT-002) with no suffix patterns. CBM-redo will go directly to the v1.1 mechanism. An automated rewrite would either (i) require operator review per-record (parent-child direction isn't always recoverable from a suffix alone), violating the additive-only posture; or (ii) make heuristic guesses that operators would have to correct, adding friction without value. Leaving legacy records untouched preserves v0.4-era data verbatim; explicit edges are added as operators encounter the records.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Automated rewrite scanning `entity_name` for variant-pattern punctuation (rejected; parent-child direction not always unambiguously recoverable). One-pass migration emitting a report of suspected patterns for operator triage (rejected for v1.1; no production data needs it). Lazy migration: prompt operator in UI on first access to matching records (rejected; UX surface no operator has asked for and the data doesn't exist to need).
decisions.consequences: v0.4 records carry forward with `entity_kind = NULL` and zero variant edges. CBM-redo Phase 1 captures variants directly using the v1.1 mechanism. If a later engagement has accreted legacy suffix-named variants between v0.4 and v1.1, operators handle the migration manually via the UI's reference-create dialog. No data-migration script is shipped with migration 0019.
